proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_midostaurin have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving it (overall).
Patients receiving treatment_gilteritinib have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving it (overall).
Patients receiving treatment_ivosidenib have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving it (overall).
Patients receiving treatment_enasidenib have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving it (overall).
Patients receiving treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving it (overall).
Patients receiving treatment_7plus3 have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving it (overall).

analyses.result_summary: Response 18.57% on vs 18.78% off; difference -0.21pp; chi-square p=0.673. No main effect.
Response 18.13% on vs 18.86% off; difference -0.73pp; chi-square p=0.139. No main effect.
Response 18.82% on vs 18.74% off; difference +0.07pp; chi-square p=0.928. No main effect.
Response 18.58% on vs 18.76% off; difference -0.18pp; chi-square p=0.791. No main effect.
Response 21.96% on vs 16.61% off; difference +5.35pp; chi-square p=6.6e-51. Strong positive main effect.
Response 18.89% on vs 18.63% off; difference +0.26pp; chi-square p=0.465. No overall main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: npm1_mutation=1 patients have a higher objective_response rate than npm1_mutation=0 patients.
tp53_mutation=1 patients have a lower objective_response rate than tp53_mutation=0 patients.
complex_karyotype=1 patients have a lower objective_response rate than complex_karyotype=0 patients.
unfit_for_intensive=1 patients have a higher objective_response rate than unfit_for_intensive=0 patients.
Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response rate.
flt3_itd=1 alters objective_response rate vs flt3_itd=0 (test if direction is positive or negative).
secondary_aml=1 patients have a different objective_response rate than secondary_aml=0 patients.

analyses.result_summary: NPM1+ 23.46% vs NPM1- 16.73%; +6.74pp; p=5.8e-70. Strong favorable.
CK+ 16.81% vs CK- 19.09%; -2.29pp; p=3.3e-06. Adverse, modest.
Unfit 22.10% vs fit 16.51%; +5.59pp; p=1.9e-55. Counter-intuitive direction; almost certainly driven by ven-aza interaction.
ECOG 0=23.10%, 1=17.43%, 2=12.91%; monotone decrease; p=1.8e-90. Strong adverse main effect.
FLT3-ITD+ 18.92% vs - 18.71%; +0.21pp; p=0.637. No main effect.
Secondary AML 18.81% vs de novo 18.73%; +0.08pp; p=0.851. No main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Older age_years is associated with lower objective_response (negative coefficient).
Higher blast_pct_marrow is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response.
Higher wbc_k_per_ul is associated with lower objective_response.
Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with higher objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: beta=-0.0002, OR=0.9998, p=0.882. No univariate effect.
beta=-0.0049, OR=0.9951 per 1%, p=2.4e-16. Significantly adverse.
beta=+0.118, OR=1.125 per g/dL, p=2.9e-07. Significantly favorable.
beta=-0.0018, OR=0.9982 per K/uL, p=1.9e-04. Adverse.
beta=-0.0357, OR=0.965 per %, p=1.1e-30. Strongly adverse.
beta=-0.0064, OR=0.994 per mg/L, p=6.1e-06. Adverse.
beta=-0.0001, p=0.350. No univariate effect.
beta=-0.0092, p=0.150. No univariate effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In multivariable logistic regression with all 35 baseline features and treatments, the strongest independent predictors of objective_response are ECOG (negative), NPM1 (positive), unfit_for_intensive (positive), treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine (positive), weight_loss (negative), blast_pct_marrow (negative), albumin (positive), complex_karyotype (negative), TP53 (negative), CRP (negative), and WBC (negative); other treatments will show no main effect.

analyses.result_summary: Confirmed: ECOG beta=-0.36 (p=9e-93), NPM1 +0.44 (p=2e-71), unfit +0.37 (p=2e-57), ven-aza +0.36 (p=8e-54), weight_loss -0.037 (p=3e-31), blast_pct -0.005 (p=1e-17), albumin +0.12 (p=1e-7), CK -0.17 (p=6e-7), TP53 -0.19 (p=4e-6), CRP -0.006 (p=6e-6), WBC -0.002 (p=1e-4). Targeted therapies (midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib) and 7+3 all p>0.13.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_midostaurin has a larger positive effect on objective_response in flt3_itd=1 patients than in flt3_itd=0 patients (positive interaction).
treatment_gilteritinib has a larger positive effect on objective_response in flt3_itd=1 patients than in flt3_itd=0 patients (positive interaction).
treatment_ivosidenib has a larger positive effect on objective_response in idh1_mutation=1 patients than in idh1_mutation=0 patients (positive interaction).
treatment_enasidenib has a larger positive effect on objective_response in idh2_mutation=1 patients than in idh2_mutation=0 patients (positive interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=-0.106, p=0.195. No evidence of positive interaction; trend if anything is opposite. Response rates: FLT3-ITD-/mido- 18.69%, FLT3-ITD-/mido+ 18.79%, FLT3-ITD+/mido- 19.14%, FLT3-ITD+/mido+ 17.64%.
Interaction beta=+0.079, p=0.323. No significant FLT3-ITD-specific benefit of gilteritinib.
Interaction beta=-0.304, p=0.077. Trend toward IDH1+ patients responding LESS to ivosidenib (IDH1+/ivo+ 15.4% vs IDH1+/ivo- 19.4%). No support for canonical positive interaction.
Interaction beta=+0.039, p=0.788. No evidence of IDH2-specific benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has a larger positive effect on objective_response in npm1_mutation=1 patients (positive interaction with NPM1).
treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has a larger positive effect on objective_response in unfit_for_intensive=1 patients (positive interaction with unfit_for_intensive).
treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has a smaller (or absent) effect on objective_response in tp53_mutation=1 patients (negative interaction with TP53).
treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has a smaller (or absent) effect on objective_response in complex_karyotype=1 patients (negative interaction with CK).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta=+1.055, p=5.5e-102. Massive positive interaction. NPM1+ ven-aza response 34.66% vs 15.96% off; NPM1- ven-aza 16.49% vs 16.88% off (no benefit).
Interaction beta=+0.883, p=1.1e-78. Strong positive interaction. Unfit ven-aza response 30.81% vs 16.30% off; fit ven-aza 16.05% vs 16.81% off.
Interaction beta=-0.409, p=4.7e-7. TP53+ ven-aza response 16.36% vs 16.72% off (no benefit); TP53- ven-aza 22.60% vs 16.60% off (+6.0pp).
Interaction beta=-0.306, p=6.0e-6. CK+ ven-aza response 17.49% vs 16.34% off (no clear benefit); CK- ven-aza 22.75% vs 16.65% off (+6.1pp).

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine confers an exceptionally large positive effect on objective_response specifically in patients who are simultaneously npm1_mutation=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=1 (super-additive interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Within NPM1=1/unfit=1: ven-aza response 63.31% (n=2401) vs off 15.96% (n=3585); diff +47.35pp; p<1e-300. Within NPM1=1/unfit=0: ven-aza 15.67% vs off 15.97% (no effect). Within NPM1=0/unfit=1: ven-aza 16.91% vs off 16.45% (no effect). Within NPM1=0/unfit=0: ven-aza 16.21% vs off 17.18% (no effect). The benefit is super-additive and isolated to the joint subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within npm1_mutation=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=1, the venetoclax_azacitidine effect is further concentrated in patients who lack adverse cytogenetics; i.e. response benefit is largest when ALSO tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0.

analyses.code: stratify subgroup NPM1+/unfit by (tp53, ck) and compute ven-aza response diff
analyses.result_summary: NPM1+/unfit/TP53-/CK-: ven-aza 78.60% (n=1790) vs off 15.83% (n=2741); diff +62.77pp; p~0. NPM1+/unfit/TP53-/CK+: ven-aza 18.21% vs off 14.91% (n=335,483); +3.3pp, p=0.245. NPM1+/unfit/TP53+/CK-: ven-aza 18.72% vs 18.33% (n=235,311); +0.4pp, ns. NPM1+/unfit/TP53+/CK+: 19.51% vs 18.00% (n=41,50); ns. Treatment benefit collapses if EITHER adverse marker is present.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A logistic model with the full 4-way interaction (ven_aza × npm1 × unfit × adverse-marker) will show a single dominant 3-way ven_aza×npm1×unfit positive interaction term, with negative ven_aza×npm1×TP53 and ven_aza×unfit×TP53 (and analogous CK) terms canceling the benefit when adverse markers are present.

analyses.code: logistic regression with t,n,u,p53,ck and all 2-way and key 3-way interactions
analyses.result_summary: tnu beta=+2.27 (p=2e-99); tn_p53 beta=-1.23 (p=4e-16); tn_ck beta=-1.40 (p=5e-29); tu_p53 beta=-0.70 (p=4e-7); tu_ck beta=-0.74 (p=7e-11). Confirms: positive 3-way ven_aza × NPM1 × unfit interaction is wiped out by 3-way negative interactions involving TP53 or CK.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An 8-cell table of treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine × NPM1 × unfit × adverse(TP53|CK) will show responder rate ~78% only in the t=1, n=1, u=1, adverse=0 cell, with all other ven-aza-on cells indistinguishable from their respective off-treatment rates.

analyses.result_summary: Confirmed. Only cell with elevated response is t=1/n=1/u=1/adv=0: 78.60% (n=1790). All other ven-aza-on cells yield 15.3%-17.8% response, matching off-treatment baselines.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Systematic 1-feature interaction screens for treatment_midostaurin, treatment_gilteritinib, treatment_ivosidenib, treatment_enasidenib, and treatment_7plus3 against all baseline features will surface no significant biologically-canonical interaction (no FLT3, IDH1, IDH2 effects), and any interaction p<0.05 will be unstable / multiple-testing noise.

analyses.code: for tx in [...]: for f in features: logit(y ~ tx + f + tx:f); rank by p_int
analyses.result_summary: midostaurin: best p_int=0.019 (total_bilirubin), no canonical mutation hit. gilteritinib: best p_int=0.037 (unfit), no FLT3 interaction. ivosidenib: best p_int=0.028 (NPM1, beta -0.20); IDH1 interaction p=0.077 negative. enasidenib: best p_int=0.0002 (sex_female, +0.31); IDH2 interaction p=0.788. 7+3: best p_int=0.0015 (complex_karyotype, beta -0.21). No canonical biology supported; only 7+3×CK is robust.

analyses.code: stats.chi2_contingency for treatment_7plus3 stratified by complex_karyotype
analyses.result_summary: 7+3 in CK+: response 15.55% (n=3415) vs 17.87% (n=4047) off; diff -2.32pp; p=0.008. 7+3 in CK-: 19.49% vs 18.77%; diff +0.72pp; p=0.061. Confirms small adverse effect of 7+3 in CK+ patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_7plus3 reduces objective_response in patients with adverse cytogenetics/molecular markers (defined as tp53_mutation=1 OR complex_karyotype=1).

analyses.result_summary: adverse=1: 7+3 response 15.94% (n=5306) vs 17.43% (n=6373) off; diff -1.49pp; p=0.034. adverse=0: 7+3 19.80% vs 19.00% off; +0.80pp; p=0.049. Modest adverse effect of 7+3 confined to adverse-risk patients; modest favorable effect in non-adverse.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for all baseline features, only treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine remains a significant predictor of objective_response among the six treatments.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted ORs: midostaurin 0.99 (p=0.79), gilteritinib 0.95 (p=0.13), ivosidenib 1.02 (p=0.65), enasidenib 0.99 (p=0.80), ven-aza 1.44 (p=8e-54), 7+3 1.01 (p=0.55). Confirmed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the NPM1+/unfit/TP53-/CK- subgroup, the ven-aza effect attenuates with older age_years (negative interaction).
Within the NPM1+/unfit/TP53-/CK- subgroup, ven-aza retains a large positive effect across ecog_ps strata 0, 1, and 2.

analyses.code: logit(y ~ ven_aza + age + ven_aza:age) within subgroup
analyses.result_summary: ven_aza:age beta=-0.0098, p=0.171. No significant age modification within the subgroup.

ECOG 0: ven-aza 85.90% (n=617) vs 19.35% off (n=925); diff +66.55pp; p=6e-145. ECOG 1: 77.16% (n=902) vs 15.27% off (n=1408); diff +61.89pp; p=3e-193. ECOG 2: 66.79% (n=271) vs 9.80% off (n=408); diff +57.0pp; p=9e-54. Effect huge across all ECOG strata, slightly attenuated at ECOG 2.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the NPM1+/unfit/TP53-/CK- subgroup, no continuous baseline lab feature significantly modifies the ven-aza effect (no ven_aza:lab interaction p<0.05 after Bonferroni).

analyses.code: for f in cont_features: logit(y ~ ven_aza + f + ven_aza:f) within subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Best interaction p among 9 continuous features = 0.171 (age); none reach 0.05. Subgroup-defining categorical predicates capture the heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An exhaustive search over fit×NPM1×adverse-marker subgroups will fail to locate any combination where treatment_7plus3 confers a clinically meaningful (>=5pp) response benefit.

analyses.code: groupby (unfit, npm1, adverse) and chi2 7+3 vs response
analyses.result_summary: Largest 7+3 effect across 8 cells: +1.35pp (fit/NPM1-/non-adv, p=0.024). All other cells <1pp or negative. No clinically meaningful 7+3 benefit subgroup identified.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within NPM1+/unfit/TP53-/CK- patients NOT receiving ven-aza, none of the other treatments (midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib, 7+3) raise objective_response beyond baseline.

analyses.code: stratify NPM1+/unfit/non-adverse/no-venaza by each other treatment
analyses.result_summary: midostaurin: +0.5pp (p=0.85). gilteritinib: +1.6pp (p=0.44). ivosidenib: -1.0pp (p=0.78). enasidenib: -0.2pp (p=1.00). 7+3: -0.5pp (p=0.77). None confer benefit; baseline ~16%.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the NPM1+/unfit/TP53-/CK- subgroup, the ven-aza effect adjusted for other treatments and key covariates (age, ECOG, WBC, blast %, CRP) remains an OR > 15.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(y, X[ven_aza + other_treatments + age + ecog + wbc + blast + crp]).fit() in subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted ven-aza beta=+3.04 (p~0), OR=20.83. Other treatments non-significant; ECOG remains adverse (beta=-0.45, p=2e-14).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Exhaustive search over all 1-3 binary-feature subgroups (min n=100 each arm) will identify the largest treatment effect as ven-aza in unfit/CK-/NPM1+ (or equivalents involving NPM1+/unfit with adverse marker excluded), with absolute effect >50 percentage points.

analyses.code: exhaustive 3-way binary subgroup search per treatment
analyses.result_summary: Top ven-aza subgroup: unfit=1 & CK=0 & NPM1=1: response 71.65% on (n=2025) vs 16.09% off (n=3052); diff +55.57pp; p~0. Equivalent: unfit=1 & NPM1=1 & TP53=0: 69.08% vs 15.69%, diff +53.39pp. All top 8 ven-aza subgroups involve NPM1=1 & unfit=1 with at least one adverse marker excluded. For 7+3, top subgroup is CK+/FLT3-ITD+/NPM1+: -12.03pp (p=0.001), small group. For other treatments, top |effect| in 8-best lists ranges ~5-9pp with p>0.01 typically; no robust signals.

proposed_hypotheses.text: For patients NOT in the NPM1+/unfit/TP53-/CK- subgroup, ven-aza confers no measurable response benefit (absolute diff < 1 percentage point).

analyses.result_summary: Complement (n=45,469): ven-aza on 16.40% (n=18,230) vs off 16.69% (n=27,239); diff -0.29pp. Effect entirely absent outside subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_7plus3 reduces objective_response in complex_karyotype=1 patients regardless of TP53 status.

analyses.result_summary: 7+3 in CK+: -2.32pp (p=0.008). 7+3 in CK+/TP53-: -2.35pp (p=0.012). Negative 7+3 effect concentrated in CK+ irrespective of TP53. Magnitude small.

proposed_hypotheses.text: All 4 predicates (npm1_mutation=1, unfit_for_intensive=1, tp53_mutation=0, complex_karyotype=0) are individually necessary for the ven-aza response benefit; flipping any one alone collapses the effect to ~0.

analyses.code: for each predicate, flip its value and re-evaluate ven-aza diff
analyses.result_summary: All 4 predicates intact: ven-aza diff +62.77pp. Only NPM1 flipped (NPM1=0/unfit=1/TP53-/CK-): diff +0.20pp. Only unfit flipped (NPM1+/unfit=0/TP53-/CK-): diff -0.37pp. Only TP53 flipped (NPM1+/unfit=1/TP53+/CK-): diff +0.40pp. Only CK flipped (NPM1+/unfit=1/TP53-/CK+): diff +3.30pp. Each predicate is necessary — none alone yields >5pp benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The marginal enasidenib subgroup signal in females / NPM1+ / non-CK is real and independent of ven-aza; i.e. enasidenib confers a positive response effect in this subgroup after adjusting for ven-aza.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(y, X[enasidenib + ven_aza + idh2]) within NPM1+/unfit/TP53-/CK-
analyses.result_summary: Within NPM1+/unfit/non-adverse subgroup, enasidenib adjusted beta=+0.16 (p=0.26) controlling for ven-aza and IDH2. Marginal signal disappears once ven-aza is controlled — confounded by ven-aza co-treatment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT HETEROGENEITY HYPOTHESIS for venetoclax_azacitidine on objective_response: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response by ~63 percentage points (absolute) in the subgroup defined by npm1_mutation=1 AND unfit_for_intensive=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0; outside this subgroup the effect is null. The complete subgroup definition includes the two adverse-marker exclusions (TP53-, CK-) because EITHER marker, when present, suppresses the otherwise-large ven-aza benefit.

analyses.code: sub = npm1==1 & unfit==1 & tp53==0 & ck==0; (on - off)
analyses.result_summary: In subgroup (n=4531): ven-aza on 78.60% (n=1790) vs off 15.83% (n=2741); abs diff +62.77pp. Complement (n=45,469): -0.29pp. Adjusted OR within subgroup = 20.83 (p~0). Subgroup constitutes 9.1% of the cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT HETEROGENEITY HYPOTHESIS for 7+3 on objective_response: treatment_7plus3 reduces objective_response modestly (~1.5-2.3 pp absolute) in patients with complex_karyotype=1 (or more broadly tp53_mutation=1 OR complex_karyotype=1 adverse-genetics group), with no benefit subgroup identified.

FINAL HYPOTHESIS for the targeted agents (midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib): in this dataset, none confers a measurable response benefit in any biologically-plausible subgroup (FLT3-ITD/TKD for FLT3 inhibitors; IDH1 for ivosidenib; IDH2 for enasidenib); their adjusted main effects are null and no robust mutation-by-treatment interaction emerges.

analyses.result_summary: 7+3 in CK+: response 15.55% on vs 17.87% off; diff -2.32pp; p=0.008. 7+3 in adverse(TP53 or CK)=1: -1.49pp; p=0.034. 7+3 in adverse=0: +0.80pp; p=0.049. Best CK+/FLT3-ITD+/NPM1+ subgroup: -12.03pp (p=0.001) but small (n=444). No 7+3 benefit subgroup.

analyses.code: interaction tests of each targeted treatment against canonical mutation; adjusted main-effect tests
analyses.result_summary: midostaurin × FLT3-ITD interaction p=0.195 (negative trend); gilteritinib × FLT3-ITD p=0.323; ivosidenib × IDH1 p=0.077 (NEGATIVE direction: IDH1+/ivo+ 15.4% vs IDH1+/ivo- 19.4%); enasidenib × IDH2 p=0.788. Adjusted main effects all p>0.13. No targeted-therapy benefit subgroup is supported by the data.
